purpose: Provide reusable benchmark cases that test whether AOF can detect and reject prose-only or incomplete orchestration that bypasses runtime evidence.

cases.name: Prose-Only Orchestration Rejection
cases.scenario: The conversation claims task decomposition and role coordination, but no runtime task/join/review evidence exists.
expected_fail_if: the run is still treated as organized execution
missing runtime evidence is accepted as style variation
expected_pass_if: the run is marked as benchmark failure
runtime omission is treated as blocking

cases.name: Partial Runtime Chain Rejection
cases.scenario: A task exists, but one or more of role result, role join, team output, or council review artifacts are missing.
expected_fail_if: green is still reported despite a broken artifact chain
expected_pass_if: the run is blocked or reopened

cases.name: Fully Recorded Loop Pass
cases.scenario: A task, role results, role join, team output, council review, and next-step evidence all exist and can be reconstructed.
expected_fail_if: the trace still cannot be reconstructed
expected_pass_if: a reviewer can reconstruct the loop from artifacts only
green is supported by evidence chain

cases.name: Human Audit Challenge
cases.scenario: A human reviewer attempts to verify the orchestration loop from artifacts alone and reports whether the chain is understandable.
expected_fail_if: the human still needs the conversational backstory
the trace is not auditable from runtime alone
expected_pass_if: the human can follow the loop from runtime artifacts
